programme.desc: The last remaining bakers make a perfect picnic, a summer classic and a baked celebration of our planet. Who will be crowned the winner of Bake Off 2022?

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew and Tee visit Wookey Hole near Somerset;

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc: In the penultimate programme, Gordon returns to La Riviera - now renamed Abstract - a fine dining restaurant in Inverness. Owned by multi-millionaire Barry Larson and costing £8,000 a week to run, the place boasted top French chef Loic LeFebvre and an impeccably-trained kitchen staff, all of them on a mission to bring sophisticated French cooking to the home of the haggis.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Somewhere on Earth is there a massive whirlpool sucking whole ships and their hapless crews to a watery grave? Can a passing wintery road sweeper displace so much air that it flips an oncoming car?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Richard has come to A&E with sore joints. Stanlie is airlifted to hospital after being impaled on a metal rod. Max and his 2-year-old daughter Darcy have been involved in a bus collision.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.sub-title: From Union Inn to Elysian Fields
programme.desc:
The competition begins at the Union Inn in Cowes on the Isle of Wight. The guests then head across the island to Shanklin where Andy Stein and Jo Rachy run the Shoreside Inn. A heated argument breaks out during the afternoon and breakfast leaves them cold.
The third visit is to Raj's Kirribilli Guesthouse in Newquay, Cornwall. Last is the Elysian Fields B&B in Helston, Cornwall where Jackie Burchell showers her guests with gifts including personalised lollies.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Nicole has burnt her foot with caustic soda. Francis, 28, fell off his bike and is suffering amnesia.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
Hazel's Roost in Bainbridge, Yorkshire, goes first, with Millie Bussey trying to impress her guests with some breakfast magic.
At Rhandregynwen Hall in Llanymynech, Wales, the guests go buggy racing. The third visit is to the Smart and Simple Hotel in Tunbridge Wells, Kent, managed by fake tan enthusiast Simon Colbran. During dinner, one guest unwittingly insults another, who vows revenge.
The last stop is to Pearfield - Cathy Robinson's B&B in Petersfield, West Sussex. Here the homely vibe isn't well received by everyone.

programme.title: Clarkson's Farm
programme.desc: Spring has sprung and rejuvinated Jeremy's farming the unfarmed idea, as he takes on multiple new projects to try and turn a profit from his unused land.. And there are some one week old arrivals to the farm to lift everyone's spirits.

programme.title: Richard Hammond's BIG
programme.desc: Richard explores the huge Tottenham Hotspur Football Stadium in London. He explores a pitch that splits and rolls away and takes a terrifying walk on the stadium roof!

programme.title: Mind Field
programme.desc: How much of the sensations we feel is determined by our physical bodies? Maybe our minds play a bigger role than we know. I’ll see if people can be tricked into feeling intense physical pain, even though it’s all in their heads. I’ll also look at a machine that makes it possible for you to tickle yourself, and I’ll show you a weird physical illusion you can do at home.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.sub-title: From Avenue Hotel to One 4 Two
programme.desc:
The Avenue Hotel in Brockhall, Lancashire, is first to host, with Margaret Lofthouse and Janet Sutton banking on their northern charm appeasing any disgruntled guests.
In Penrith, Cumbria, Mary and Graham Walker run the Llamas Pyjamas, with their love of llamas extending to themed sleeping quarters.
At the Kallacliff in Newquay, Cornwall, newbies Liz Hall and Colin Eadie are still learning the B&B business, and the final B&B is the One 4 Two in Bude, Cornwall - a £230-a-night love nest set up by Kathy Lawrence.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Dave, who is 56 and a builder, has fallen 15 feet from a ladder onto concrete. 26-year-old Finn has been hit by a car while he was out jogging. 3-year-old Alf has also been hit by a car while waiting to cross the road.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Automatic Sliding Doors, Gin, Firearms Restoration
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for Automatic Sliding Doors, Gin, and Firearms Restoration and shows how each is made.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.sub-title: From Edenmore Guest House to The George and Dragon
programme.desc:
The Edenmore Guest House in Ardrossan, Scotland, is first to host, with owner Hugh Begbie suffering negative feedback about his B&B's exterior, while party-loving landlords Ian and Elle Betchley take a quick dislike to Andrew and Jeanette Collette.
The second visit is to Diane Lowe's Grassington Lodge in Yorkshire, where Hugh Begbie find's Diane's establishment too snobby.
Tensions rise at Manor Farm in Oxfordshire, with Ian and Elle comparing staying at Andrew and Jeanette Collette's B&B to a trip to your 'mother-in-law'.
At the final B&B, the George and Dragon in Buckinghamshire, tears flow at dinner.

programme.title: Brain Games
programme.desc: Your face is pretty remarkable. Whether you've got a baby face, a poker face, or a face only a mother could love... your identity is deeply rooted in your own unique face. And with over 40 muscles twisting and contorting, your face allows you to express a near infinite number of emotions and feelings. But not only are you capable of making faces -- your brain is also highly adept at recognizing what other peoples' faces are saying to you. In this episode about faces, we're going to mess with your mind and show you that there's more to a face than meets the eye.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Paul, 60, has been in a collision with a van. Jeremy, who is 4 and has a series of chronic conditions including microcephaly and epilepsy, is struggling to breathe.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Jamie and Adam try to scientifically explain the recently publicized and highly entertaining chemical reaction caused as a result of placing a Mentos candy into a 2-liter bottle of Diet Coke. The rest of the Mythbusters team explores the possibility of a standard postage stamp causing enough imbalance in the rotating assemblies of a helicopter to cause it to crash.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Jungles cover roughly three percent of our planet yet contain fifty percent of the world's species. Experience the countless flora and fauna that inhabit these unique areas.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.sub-title: From 20 Albany Street to Wolds Village
programme.desc:
The competition begins in Edinburgh at 20 Albany Street, run by Denise Walker.
The second visit is to Tom and Angela Walker's Highland Guest House in Callandar, Scotland, where after a testing afternoon of highland dancing, battle lines are drawn.
In Skegness, at Kath Milward's Fairfax Hotel, the guests are left shocked by her early departure at dinner.
At Wolds Village in Yorkshire owners Chris and Sally Brealey are left fuming after getting mixed feedback.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob and Paul start the series by fishing the beautiful river Exe in Devon, looking for Grilse, also known as the ‘summer silver’. After a day of limited success, they enjoy a game of cricket before having a go at some shore fishing at Bossington Beach in Somerset.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave finds a very rare 70s Sunbeam Lotus that has been rotting away in a farmyard for 29 years, while a Morris Minor convertible goes under the hammer.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew and Tee visit Forde Abbey in Chard; The Mill Toy and Pedal Car Museum in Northiam; Father/son antique shops in Margate

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: On this episode the guys ask, can a car stereo destroy a car with just sound? Adam and Jamie pump up the bass to find out. And the Tory, Kari, and Grant hit the road to take on two tall tales from down under.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.sub-title: From Langsmeade Guesthouse to Five Bells
programme.desc:
In Thame, Oxfordshire, at Langsmeade Guesthouse Marianne Aben and housekeeper Fran Birchwood are both pleased and bemused with their guests' feedback.
The second visit is to family run Stratford House in Stratford-upon-Avon, where some guests aren't impressed with hosts Natasha and Dai Pollock's children being present at breakfast.
At the Edmund Guest House in Deal, Kent, tensions rise between Mark Genders and hosts Michael and Lorna Prime.
The strain on the group is magnified on the final visit to the Five Bells in Berkshire, when Michael and Lorna are underwhelmed by Mark Genders pub B&B.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.sub-title: From Trecarne House to A Room with a View
programme.desc:
The competition kicks off in Liskeard, Cornwall at Trecarne House with hosts Trish and Tom Brock-Morgan.
The second visit is to the B&B boat the Magna Carta in Windsor run by Captain Dominic Read.
Trish and Rayne's tea tray feud continues at Ash Cottage in Kent.
The final visit is to A Room with a View in Brighton owned by Stephen Bull.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Scuba Lights, Interchangeable Sandals, Race Car Simulators, Fibreglass Doors
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for Scuba Diving Lights, Interchangeable Sandals, Race Car Simulators, and Fibreglass Doors and shows how each is made.

programme.title: Brain Games
programme.desc: Do you sometimes hear a little voice in your head, an inner critic that keeps you from performing at your best? What if we told you there’s a way to change what that voice is telling you that will improve your performance in just about anything you try? It might come as a surprise, but research shows thinking positively is more than just a warm and fuzzy idea. And in this episode about positive thinking, we’re going to show you how thinking positively can change your thoughts, your reality and even your life! We’re going to mess with your mood to show you what really makes that glass seem half empty or half full; why people who stand tall, stand happy; and how to turn that frown upside down.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.sub-title: From Nateby Inn to Ammonite Lodge
programme.desc:
At the Nateby Inn in Nateby, Cumbria, Gaynor Walker hopes her immaculate rooms and northern charm will impress her guests.
The B&Bers then head to Outfields Farm in Bedfordshire where Graham and Julia Ashley have a surprise for their fellow competitors.
The third visit is to At Home B&B in Chiswick, west London where artist Bob Osborne's irreverent attitude and casual approach to cleanliness causes confusion.
The last stop is to the Ammonite Lodge Guest House in Chard, Somerset.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: It's "lights, camera, action!" time again for the MythBusters, as they test more Hollywood heists. This time, our cat burglars Adam and Jamie go cold as they try to beat a heat-detecting alarm sensor.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc:
In the final programme, Gordon returns to Momma Cherri's Soul Food Shack, an intimate 40-seater in Brighton where he oversaw one of the most spectacular changes of fortune at a single venue. Owner Charita Jones was producing a menu of irresistible unique classics from the Deep South, but at the same facing financial disaster. The punters were missing, and so was her business acumen. But Gordon succeeded in shutting her out of the kitchen, getting the chef back on the boil and putting the soul back into the business.
Today Charita is fully-booked round the clock and bursting out of the Soul Shack to take on the 110-seater Momma Cherri's Big House. But expansion brings a different set of challenges, and she's still struggling to control her staff and manage her success. Has she got what it takes to make the next step up, or will she be forced to rethink her grand plans?

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Cake Week, and a new batch of bakers tackle perfect vertical layers, an iconic chocolate cake and a showstopping sponge menagerie of animals, to showcase their architectural skills.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.sub-title: From Abbey House to the Old Farmhouse
programme.desc:
At the Abbey House in Penrith, Cumbria, Ian and Samantha Sugden pride themselves on their simple but scrupulously clean rooms.
The rivals then travel to Compton House in Newark, Nottinghamshire, where sisters Lisa Holloway and Becky Waltman offer antique filled rooms and five star breakfasts.
The third visit is to the White Horse Inn in Ripon, Yorkshire managed by Dave and Jo Bate. Their basic rooms, and pub breakfasts don't impress though.
Last is the Old Farmhouse, Chester, run by retired teachers Ed and Jane Mitchell.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike takes the ultimate plunge and invests in a car - and a boat, all in one. It’s the Amphicar, an amphibious vehicle that Edd has been dreaming of for years. But when he peels back the layers and discovers the car’s problems are serious, will he find himself in deep water and rinse away all the profit? This challenge truly puts Mike and Edd’s skills to the test and could see them literally sink or swim!

programme.title: Clarkson's Farm
programme.desc: Jeremy's mushroom empire begins to appear all consuming as he struggles to keep on top of his newest venture and Kaleb and Charlie are off to London for a very important meeting.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.sub-title: From Knockaloe Beg Farm to Sheepy Lodge
programme.desc:
The competition begins at Knockaloe Beg Farm in Peel on the Isle of Man. Here John and Fiona Anderson treat their guests to laser games, a trip around their farm and local produce.
The guests then head across the island to Port Erin where Phil Routledge runs the Arches B&B.
The third visit is to Robbie and John Welling's The Trafford Hotel in Blackpool.
Last is Sheepy Lodge in Sheepy Magna, Warwickshire where Julie Brogan brought up her family and now fills it with paying guests.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Wood Windows, Woven Cashmere Fabric, Plastic Recycling Machines, Architectural Glass
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for Wood Windows, Woven Cashmere Fabric, Plastic Recycling Machines, and Architectural Glass and shows how each is made.

programme.title: Look Around You
programme.desc: The team looks at advances in food science and technology, and the world's one and only vegetable orchestra, led by Teddy Clarke, gives us a live performance in the studio. We meet Andy Gough, Slimmer of the Year, a fellow who lost fifty-two stone [for American viewers: one stone is sixteen pounds] in six weeks, thanks to a controversial slimming powder. We also visit the food industry's annual show and see some inspiring hi-tech developments. A special report looks at the growth of fast food casserole joints, stepping into the world's only computer-run casserole cafe where the menu features four types of casserole - beef, chicken, ham and porcupine. Lastly, the gang throws a futuristic birthday party for Pam - with a surprise guest.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.sub-title: From Dunsandles Guesthouse to Heyford House
programme.desc:
The competition begins in New Brighton, Merseyside, where Andy Johnson and Dave Owen run Dunsandles Guesthouse.
The guests then head to the waterways of Lancashire to board the Lady Teal Hotel Boat owned by Nick and Gina Mead.
The third visit is to Brock Burgius' York House B&B in Tetbury, Gloucestershire.
Finally the B&Bers visit Heyford House in Lower Heyford, Oxfordshire run by Sonya and Leo Brooke-Little.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg joins the Adam West High basketball team, and her play is fueled by Peter's lambasting from the crowd. Meanwhile, Stewie goes down a rabbit hole of conspiracies, which ultimately ends with him developing multiple personalities.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter’s sister, Karen, decides to come for Thanksgiving and bullies Peter the same way Meg treats him. While Meg is at first amused, she later feels badly and decides to help Peter train to defeat his sister in a wrestling match. Meanwhile, to avoid overeating on Thanksgiving, Stewie and Brian decide to go on a cleanse, which Stewie takes one step too far.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter makes it through a bad flu season without getting sick, the hospital asks him to volunteer as a nurse, but he finds himself at a crossroads when his mortal enemy, The Giant Chicken, is admitted to the ER. Meanwhile, Brian receives his genetic test results and learns he is part cat, causing him to have an identity crisis.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The guys try to help Mayor Wild West reconnect with his estranged father, Old West.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When the owner of The Drunken Clam is killed, Peter and the guys must find a way to save the bar, after the bank threatens to close it. Meanwhile, Meg gets a part-time job at a funeral home.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois becomes the leader of a group of mothers who want to ban books from schools.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter's friends roast him. With his feelings hurt, he seeks refuge by befriending a group of gossipy women.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins tell their own versions of three HBO shows: "Game of Thrones," "Succession" and "Big Little Lies."

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois decide to open a cookie store, but Peter ruins things when he decides to bring clients in his own way.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Hard Times at Adam West High
programme.desc: Chris uses Stewie's time machine to stop himself from having an erection during a book report. Peter becomes jealous when he sits out of a lottery pool and the guys win.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In an anthology episode, the cast reenacts three classic pieces of literature commonly taught in high school: "The Great Gatsby," "The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn" and "Of Mice and Men".

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie ties the knot with his old flame Olivia, but their relationship quickly turns, and becomes normal dull marriage. It all escalates when they go on a double date with Brian and Jillian. Meanwhile, Lois introduces Peter to chick flicks, so he decides to make his own movie with a plot based on every chick movie ever.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie gets a mail order bride from Ukraine and experiences domestic life. Meanwhile, Peter and Chris become addicted to free hotel breakfast buffets.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Stewie's new best friend falls ill, Lois takes him to the hospital where she finds out he has a critical, but treatable, disease. However, the boy's parents refuse treatment due to their religious beliefs, leaving Lois no choice but to take drastic measures to seek treatment for the boy herself. Meanwhile, Peter has a religious experience of his own.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After a yacht race that the Griffins win through disobeying Lois' father, Lois decides to fulfill her dream of being a model, which her father never allowed. After she begins partying and using drugs, it is up to Peter and Lois' father to bring her back.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and the guys become shipwrecked at Fire Island; Chris and Brian perform a lobotomy on a social-media-crazed Stewie.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter swaps lives with Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh and is forced to make a ruling on the constitutionality of gay marriage, meanwhile, Brett tries to become friends with Quagmire, Cleveland and Joe.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian become overwhelmed after winning a coffee shop. Peter and his friends annoy Meg at her new lifeguard job when they don't follow the pool rules.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire admits that he is in love with Lois. Unfortunately, he proclaims his love while he has accidentally pocket-dialed Peter. Peter is furious and Brian takes it upon himself to fix the situation.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To take advantage of a work policy granting leave in the event of a pet's death, Peter adopts the oldest bird he can find... and realizes it used to belong to Hitler. Meanwhile, after giving Stewie a signed copy of his book, Brian finds it in a free library, causing Brian to give up writing.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To celebrate Peter and Lois' wedding anniversary, the Griffins present their own versions of three well-known love stories.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter gets carried away when the Nielsen people select the Griffins to have their television-viewing habits monitored and he steals a number of the Nielsen boxes in hopes of controlling the airwaves.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: At his high school reunion, Peter tries to impress his fellow classmates. When Peter, heading for the bathroom, charges through the crowd of people, impresses quarterback Tom Brady, who offers Peter a spot on the New England Patriots. Meanwhile, Brian owes Stewie money, after Stewie becomes a sports bookie.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In search of Stewie's cherished big wheel tricycle, that Peter threw out, Stewie and Brian embark on an adventure to "Garbage Island," a giant floating mass of trash out in the sea. Meanwhile, Lois tries to help Quagmire elevate his dating game, as he courts a middle-aged woman.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter takes his family on a road trip to Bend, Oregon to go to the last Blockbuster so they can have a movie night. But the Griffins road trip leads to disastrous consequences for them after Peter is unable to pay a late fee on a movie he has forgotten to return and the family gets chased by Blockbuster employees.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: A Few More Ways to Die in the West
programme.desc: In this Griffin family spoof of the Old West, Peter, known as Quiet Burp, and his gang of outlaws agrees to escort a wealthy businessman on a train journey.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Ryan Reynolds pays a visit to Quahog and becomes obsessed with Peter Griffin. Meanwhile, Stewie tries to be a big boy and steals the family car for a joy ride but lands in a bit of trouble.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter learns from a psychic through a palm reading that he had a fascinating past life in the 17th century as Griffin Peterson, a dignified English gentleman who vied for the love of Lady Redbush in the newly founded American colony of Quahog.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Brian has a near-death experience in the hospital, he decides to lead a new life. He ends up getting a nightly singing gig with Frank Sinatra Jr., but his new lifestyle doesn't last very long. Meanwhile, Meg, after joining a new after-school group, pretends to be a lesbian.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Are You There God? It's Me, Peter
programme.desc: After Peter falls into a coma, Peter meets God face-to-face, which leads him to ask some of life's tough questions.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie tries to civilize his half-brother, Boston Stewie, so that he may be adopted. Meanwhile, Meg and Chris start a venture in which people online pay Chris to sit on and smash cupcakes.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is mistaken for being a single parent, and relishes in the perks that come with it.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg agrees to be Bruce and Jeffery's surrogate causing a pregnant Meg to annoy her family. But, when the couple fails to pick up the baby, the Griffins must take care of their new family.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire meets an avid dog lover, and pretends Brian is his dog in an attempt to win her over. Meanwhile, Chris helps Stewie track down the culprit who stole his Halloween candy.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After going on a date with a Cuban-American woman named Carmen, Brian inexplicably develops a Cuban accent- which he worries will hurt his chances with her.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The guys recount their first true love stories, leading to parodies of "Castaway," "Dirty Dancing" and various Meg Ryan rom-coms.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Meg suddenly becomes internet famous, she and an excited Peter work together to build her following and create content for her new image that revolves around unhealthy eating. Meanwhile, Brian and Stewie revel in a new ride while Brian's car gets repaired.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris is forbidden to see Jerome's daughter Pam, so when the kids run away together, Jerome and Peter must work together to find them.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter realizes Joe has never found him funny, he is determined to make Joe laugh; Lois chaperones Chris and Meg's school trip, where she strives to reunite the principal with his long-lost love.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter becomes friends with a cool, handsome paramedic named Stryker Foxx. Wrapped up in his newfound "coolness," he soon dumps Quagmire, Joe and Cleveland.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg pursues an unlikely friendship with an NFL great; Stewie and Brian host a dinner party at a home furnishing store.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In an effort to improve his writing, Brian convinces Stewie to go back in time and bring Mark Twain to the present day, but the plan goes awry when Twain overindulges in pornography.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: One Foot in Front of the Mother
programme.desc: Due to a police complaint about his excessive barking, Brian has to wear a shock collar; looking to join an after-school team, Chris discovers a talent for speed walking.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Carter Pewterschmidt cancels the annual Quahog Christmas carnival, because he hates the holiday, Peter must transform his father-in-law from a Scrooge into a hometown hero in order to salvage the event. Meanwhile, Stewie devises a master plan to get the one and only thing he wants for Christmas.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg tells Ruth Brian and Stewie have been abducted after she got a phone call from the kidnappers and told her mom Lois. Meg and her friend go to Paris to solve the Mystery before it's too late to save both of them. In Paris Brian and Stewie get kidnapped, raped and held for ransom.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The guys regale each other with stories of youthful athletic accomplishments. Cleveland was a teenage baseball player from Cuba, Quagmire was a bad boy tennis superstar from New York and Peter was an underdog boxer from Philadelphia.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter, Quagmire and Joe visit the Harvard University campus, Quagmire accidentally marries a prostitute. In order to get out of his marriage, Quagmire pretends to be lovers with Peter.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Louis find themselves with a golden opportunity to reconnect as a couple after Lois' parents get a nanny to take care of the children for them.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter, Quagmire, Joe and Cleveland form a neighborhood detective agency, and are stunned to discover that Chris is a thief. Meanwhile, Lois is worried about Chris' delusional relationship with his new girlfriend, but also sees it as an opportunity for Peter to copy Chris' romantic gestures.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Peter's hair turns white from a ghost-hunting adventure in his house with the guys, his new look grabs the attention of local news anchor Tom Tucker. Under Tucker's guidance, he learns the ways of reporting fake news, and soon becomes a senior crime analyst on Quahog's Channel 5 News. Meanwhile, Brian and Stewie attempt to help Chris improve his dating game.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian discovers his nose can detect diseases and smells that someone in the Griffin house has cancer.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and Peter are divided by differing views on who the next Mayor of Quahog should be. Upset at Lois' first choice, Peter seeks out the late Mayor Adam West's cousin, Wild Wild West.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and his friends are sick of forgetting the million-dollar business ideas they come up with while drinking, so they strap on Joe's police body cams to record their every move. Meanwhile, Quagmire is trying to come to terms with an unexpected family development.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After successfully landing a plane, Brian and Quagmire go on a press tour. Meanwhile Chris and Stewie open a boutique pizza place.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins provide DVD commentary on a recent episode and reveal never-before-told drama between Peter and Lois.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Breaking Out Is Hard to Do
programme.desc: Lois starts finding herself compelled to steal things from stores, getting an almost sexual rush from breaking the law. She eventually gets caught, however, and is sent to prison. Unable to cope without her, the family decides to break her out of jail. On the run from the law, they try to make a new life for themselves while hiding out in Asiantown.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter convinces Brian to move in with his girlfriend Jillian and Stewie tags along to help pay the rent but he tries to interfere with their relationship. Meanwhile Meg and Chris get jobs at the convenience store where Chris befriends the manager Carl who leaves Meg to do all the work. Carl soon fires her and promotes Chris to the co-boss.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian's new job as a journalist leads him to uncover that Pawtucket Pat, Quahog's hero and brewery founder, took part in racist actions against Native Americans. The town becomes divided on whether or not to remove the statue that commemorates him.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Wi-Fi goes out in the Griffin house, leaving the family with nothing to do for the evening. In order to keep the family entertained, Peter and Lois decide to tell the kids the story of how they met and fell in love in the '90s.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter encourages Meg to attend a high school party, where she takes up drinking, which both concerns and delights her father. Meanwhile, Brian begins spying on his neighbors and suspects Principal Shepherd of a crime.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois embarks on a journey of self-discovery.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After a mishap at work, Peter decides to take his health more seriously and diet with the help of Quagmire, Joe, and Cleveland. When Lois takes a stab at writing a romantic fantasy novel, a curious Brian and Stewie investigate to find out who she might have a crush.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian lands a dream job at The New Yorker, but gets fired when it's revealed that he dropped out of college. Frustrated, he decides to turn his life around by going back to school and finishing his degree. Peter and his buddies, meanwhile, win an 80's TV look-alike contest dressed as the A-Team, and find that today's world still has a place for a team of renegades who can get the job done.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: On the search to find a new hobby, Chris meets a puppeteer named Franz, an old friend of Herbert's. Despite Herbert's warnings, Chris and Franz remain friends until Franz's checkered past is suddenly revealed.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter hears Loretta scream, he tries to help out, but discovers Loretta is cheating on Cleveland. Peter figures out that it was Quagmire and decides to tell Cleveland about it. But when Loretta walks out on Cleveland he doesn't take it lightly.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois becomes a stand-up comic, using real life stories about Peter as content; Peter tries his best to straighten his life out to avoid being ridiculed by Lois's act.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian get into a friendship-ending fight after Brian commits a terrible act to one of Stewie's beloved toys. Meanwhile, Peter refuses to wash his hand after meeting his favorite breakfast cereal mascot, Boo Berry.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When the Griffins win a trip to Maine at Peter's company picnic, they decide to invite their friends on the vacation. Tensions run high and things soon spiral out of control when the cabin burns down.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: The Most Interesting Man in the World
programme.desc: When Peter takes Stewie to the park for some quality father-son time, he mistakenly takes the wrong baby home, prompting Lois to call him an idiot. Determined to prove to her that he's a responsible adult, Peter goes on a business trip to Chicago and returns as an intelligent, classy person.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Cleveland returns to town, but is instantly banned from spending time with Peter when Lois and Donna get into a big fight over parenting.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: With her vision waning, Lois elects to get LASIK surgery, but exploits her new disability for monetary gain. Meanwhile, Peter, Chris and Stewie must partake in all the activities with Doug that his father neglects.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After attending obedience school, Brian becomes much more submissive, and Peter realizes he misses his old, feistier pal. Meanwhile, Chris makes a new friend at school, but feels betrayed when he discovers it was all a ploy to get closer to Meg.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: With the help of an out-of-this-world remote control, Stewie and Brian travel through alternate universes, including a post-apocalyptic world and a parallel world run by dogs where humans are pets.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter buys a farm and becomes a meth dealer.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg gets married in Russia but returns home after discovering she was used for a green card marriage. Meanwhile, Chris, in need of an extracurricular activity, joins Principal Shepherd in shooting pornos at the high school.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter makes a hologram of himself in order to get out of menial household duties.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To help promote the brewery to millennials, Peter adopts the millennial lifestyle, and his methods soon attract the attention of a high-powered Silicon Valley executive.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Pawtucket Brewery hires new bosses, who decide to change the company's mascot. In an attempt to be crowned the new face of Pawtucket Ale, Peter and Brian go head-to-head.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian is smitten with a female show dog and enters a show dog competition in which the winner gets to breed her; Peter and Lois go to extreme measures to try to convince Chris that Arthur Valentine doesn't exist.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Rob Gronkowski moves into the house behind the Griffins. Stewie raises bees and sells honey at the farmers market.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg becomes a getaway driver for Seymour, but it is soon discovered she was hallucinating after she consumed expired mustard packets and Chris is able to cover for Meg for her latest stunt. Meanwhile, Quagmire has hiccups and persuades Peter to kill him if his hiccups continue.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Impelled by nostalgia, Peter recreates movie scenes from his favorite decade; Stewie and Doug compete to retrieve a Frisbee stuck at the top of the jungle gym.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter fills in for Santa at the mall, but lets the power go to his head when he realises just what he can get away with. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian become holiday office-party crashers.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter spends more time with Meg, Lois and the Guys instead of Stewie, Brian and Chris during an apocalypse which was a later found out it was prank. Meg and Lois try to help Peter get the Chris, Stewie and Brian back after he hurt them by calling them boring.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: The Father, the Son, and the Holy Fonz
programme.desc: When Peter’s dad comes to visit, he tries to impose his religious beliefs on the family. But after a mix-up with the holy-water leaves Stewie in a bubble, Peter decides to form a church worshiping his hero, the Fonz.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: "The Bachelorette" comes to Quahog and Brian is chosen to be a contestant. Brian does not like the show, but participates just to have a vacation only to wind up falling in love with the bachelorette. Meanwhile, Chris gets a nasty pimple on his cheek, which talks to Chris and likes to cause mischief.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter gets hurt and can't work, his boss tells him he needs to speed things up. So Lois goes to the brewery to help him out. Meanwhile, Stewie finds out that Brian is dating a very attractive but not very intelligent woman. He tries to get Brian to break up with her, but Brian can't do it.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter gets himself arrested to avoid Lois' special Thanksgiving dinner; A secret that Stewie's been keeping from everyone is revealed.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: With the brewery under construction, Lois implores Peter to take a new job, but grows irate when he begins work at the grocery store. Meanwhile, armed with his police stories, Joe and Brian team up to write their own true crime novel.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and her mom, Babs, have a relaxing spa day, leaving Peter to entertain Lois’ father, Carter. The boys fall for an email scam, and have to go to Africa to get their money back. Meanwhile, Brian pursues Meg’s friend, Patty.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In this three-act parody, the Griffins spoof the stage by putting up To Kill a Mockingbird, The Odd Couple and Hamlet.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Brian sustains an injury, Lois gets hooked on his painkillers. Meanwhile, Cleveland refurbishes Peter's ping pong table, which becomes the hot spot in the neighborhood.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Vladimir Putin comes to Quahog after Peter writes to him about an alternate ending to the Russian version of Rocky IV (1985).

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Fast Times at Buddy Cianci Jr. High
programme.desc: Brian becomes a substitute teacher at Chris' school after his teacher wins the lottery. When Brian is moved to teach a class of troubled kids, Chris falls in love with his new teacher, Mrs. Lockhart, who promises to love Chris only if he kills her husband. When Lois finds out, she and Stewie decide to teach Chris a lesson.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire dates a woman named Sonja, who is just as sexually active as he is, but things take a turn for the worse when she kidnaps him to be her sex slave, so Peter, Joe and Ida must search the seedy back alleys of Quahog to rescue him. In the meantime, Brian, Stewie and Rupert get involved in a most unusual love triangle.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter accidentally discovers a missing child, he is hailed as town hero. Meanwhile, Joe quits his job and heads to Niagara Falls to live life to the fullest.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To help Chris ace his ninth grade history class, Stewie and Brian employ the time travel machine for educational purposes. Not surprisingly, all three get stuck in the past.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Take This Job and Love It
programme.desc: Peter and his friends wonder aloud about what their dream jobs would be.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Tired of not winning an Emmy, Peter embarks on his own For Your Consideration campaign for Family Guy by making the show more like Emmy-winning shows - and no category is off limits.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian writes a play that becomes a hit in Quahog, but loses his confidence when he finds that the play Stewie wrote is better than his.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg starts dating a chicken she meets on a reality dating show.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter attends a business conference in Atlantic City with Preston. Meanwhile, Stewie helps Chris prepare for his role in Adam West High's rendition of "Romeo and Juliet".

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Fed up with feeling like she doesn't belong, Meg enlists in a training program for a mission to Mars.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To everyone’s surprise, Chris wins homecoming king. However, Meg finds out that the cool kids plan to prank him during the homecoming ceremony, so they go to the school to investigate. Meanwhile, Cleveland starts hanging out with Jerome, the bartender. So Peter and the guys try to replace him.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian compete for the inheritance of a wealthy, older heiress. Meanwhile, Peter and Chris battle for Mr. Herbert's affection.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter injures himself and a family of squirrels in a chainsaw accident, Chris and Stewie take in one of the injured rodents to nurse it back to health. Meanwhile, a chiropractor fixes Peter's injury by realigning his spine - and restoring him to his "true height" of 6'5".

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire lands free plane tickets for the guys' trip to San Francisco, but they begin to doubt his flying abilities after getting a look at the auto-pilot function. While in the air, sudden events give Quagmire's skills a run for the money.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter gives Lois’ dad the bachelor party he never had, Mr. Pewterschmidt goes into a coma. Even Dr. House can't help. Lois gains control of Pewterschmidt Industries which she passes to Peter. He gets power-hungry and fires the board. Mr. Pewterschmidt bounces back and tries to regain control of what he’s built.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After running into Jesus at the Quahog Mall, Peter is stunned to discover that the Son of God is still a virgin. So, he enlists Quagmire and Cleveland to help him throw Jesus the best birthday ever by finding a way to help him become a man.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois is blocked from a vacation rental app and accused of stealing after the family's trip to Nantucket. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian buy saltwater taffy in bulk, and try their hands at door-to-door sales in the community.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Winner of a Snapple contest, Lois uses her winnings to take Meg on a tropical vacation. Flaunting their wealth, the two are kidnapped. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian undergo "friendship therapy."

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Don't Be a Dickens at Christmas
programme.desc: In this special holiday episode, a selfish Peter loses his Christmas spirit and is visited by ghosts of Christmas past, present and future who take him on a journey around Quahog in the style of Charles Dickens' "A Christmas Carol".

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The guys try to start a podcast, but Peter goes solo and branches out as a DJ. Meanwhile, back at the Griffin home, Lois' allergy forces Brian to move into Stewie's bedroom and the two have roommate difficulties.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian form a band aimed at children, but face a breakup when they set their eyes on the same girl. Meanwhile, Chris gets a job as Quagmire's personal assistant for his sex life.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie is surprised when he makes friends with another boy his age, but later taken aback when he learns that Stewie wasn't invited to his birthday party.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter tries to protest Hollywood after learning that Brad Pitt was cast in a biopic about a politician. After Pitt injures himself on set, Peter must act out the role himself.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Stewie puts on a pair of boots that make him slightly taller, his newfound confidence changes the power dynamic in the Griffin family.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Lois' piano student, Alana, comes to work for the Griffins, Lois is initially glad for the help, until she realizes Alana's intentions are more sinister than she thought.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Joe asks Peter to be the godfather to his daughter, Peter commits to his mistaken interpretation of the role so much that he becomes a real gangster.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian has a microchip that reveals he had a family before the Griffins, when he then seeks out. After meeting the richer, smarter Hendersons, Brian struggles to enjoy his life with the Griffins as much as he once did.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is given the position of the next White House Press Secretary and the Griffin family gets ready to make a move to Washington, D.C.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Doug compete for the honor of class “snack captain”.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Meg takes Stewie to the mall to meet Santa, she has a surprising response. Meanwhile, Stewie is entirely scarred from the same experience and Brian tries to help him process it.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: You May Now Kiss the... Uh... Guy Who Receives
programme.desc: Brian's gay cousin Jasper visits the Griffin house and announces that he is engaged to his partner and wants to be married at their house. When Mayor West bans gay marriage in an effort to cover up and bring attention away from a scandal of his own, Brian resorts to hostile actions to try to change the Mayor's mind. Lois and Peter question their view towards gay marriage while Chris joins the "Young Republicans" Club to impress a girl.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Meg gets turned down for a date, she asks her family for support. Lois ends up taking her to the mall, where she gets the ultimate makeover. Meanwhile, Peter tries to start a band with the guys, but when their first gig fails, the family ends up performing. Impressed by Meg's singing, a record producer signs the family, and their travels eventually land them on "Saturday Night Live."

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie "breaks up with" Rupert after learning that he used to belong to Chris. Unable to get over his feelings of betrayal, Stewie decides to take action.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Chris' Mexican girlfriend, Isabella, gets deported, he volunteers to take care of her twin babies. After learning that Isabella can't come back to the U.S., the Griffins go to Mexico to return her children.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In this follow up to the "Viewer Mail" episodes, the Griffin family retells The Lord of the Rings, explains how the Griffins' ancestors arrived in Quahog, and looks back at Quagmire's 1960s variety show.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter learns about phone apps and overloads his phone with them. He buys a new phone with more memory and gives his old phone to Chris, which causes a series of horrible events. Meanwhile, Stewie joins a tennis club and invites Brian to be his tennis partner.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Joe accidentally sees Lois naked, leading to a reinvigoration of his sex life with Bonnie. Meanwhile, Stewie is gifted an electric SUV and quickly embodies the "Range Rover" lifestyle.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When city hall threatens to close Peter's business, he joins the Tea Party and campaigns to shut down the government.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In noir-style, Peter/Mac investigates the disappearance of Meg/Sister Megan.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian and Stewie attempt to rally Quahog to try and legalize marijuana.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris leaves home to move in with Herbert while Stewie and Brian become obsessed with singer Anne Murray.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Brian's proposition to rename Quahog's high school "Adam West High," to honor the late Mayor West, is successful, he feels encouraged to run for office as Quahog's new mayor. The race takes a turn when Quagmire campaigns against Brian for the open seat.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg has fun partying with Peter when he takes her to a college for an interview. Brian must learn how to live in the wild when he gets sprayed by a skunk and is prohibited from entering the house.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Stewie sees a tag on Rupert reading "Made in China," he heads off to China to meet Rupert's family.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To pay for Stewie’s private school, Lois becomes a postal worker. She finds a letter from Peter that was never sent to his old fling, Gretchen, dated a week before their marriage. When Gretchen tries to come between Lois and Peter, Lois has a plan of her own.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffin family decides to start their own restaurant, though have a hard time attracting customers. After Joe promises to help, Peter is excited at the fact of it becoming a local hangout for the police. However, things don't really turn out as hoped, and instead it begins to be popular with the handicapped. When Peter attempts to change the place, a sudden twist of irony quickly changes his mind.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian worries about the legacy he's leaving behind, so Stewie makes a robot duplicate as a living biography.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Peter and the guys get caught pretending to be military veterans and are found guilty of the Stolen Valor Act, a judge sentences them to join the U.S. Coast Guard.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter must gain his family's respect back after he makes a humiliating throw. Brian and Stewie become chiropractors.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Death grants Peter's wish to go back in time to 1984 when he was 18, but only for one night. He ends up bailing on a date with Lois to hang out with a movie actress. When he returns to the present, he soon realizes that everything is anything but normal, as he is now married to the former actress, and Lois is with Quagmire. Meanwhile, Stewie, Chris and Meg have also taken on Quagmire's charming characteristics.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: With a newfound love for rocking out to Van Halen, Peter gets in a car accident and falls into a coma.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A powerful sleeping aid medication gives Joe the ability to walk while he is asleep, but Joe grows concerned when it becomes clear that the guys prefer his sleepwalking self.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Cooped up in a hotel room due to a big storm, the Griffins pass the time by reimagining three stories from the Bible.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg discovers the seasonal delight that is the pumpkin spice latte, but when the limited time offering lapses, she and Lois concoct an elaborate heist to steal the formula.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Stewie's day care instructor notes a lack of fatherly influence in his life, Lois insists that Peter take a more active role in raising his son. Not much works until Peter discovers how much Stewie enjoys it when Lois gets hurt, and soon the two are going to ever-greater lengths to torment and abuse her. Lois eventually puts her foot down, though, and Stewie feels betrayed when Peter agrees to knock it off. Can Peter ever make it up to him?

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter and the guys decide to go fishing, they end up caught in a storm. Stranded on a desert island, their families fear the worst. Rescued by a cruise ship months later, Peter surprises Lois at home, where he finds out that she has a new husband.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Chris take a road trip to Canada to prove the existence of Chris' girlfriend. Meanwhile, Lois and Meg compete for the attention of a handsome handyman.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois hypnotizes Peter into satisfying her sexual proclivities. Meanwhile, Stewie renovates his treehouse in an attempt to win over Brian and Chris.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A new boss quells Peter’s favorite part about work: office birthdays. Stewie is put in detention and comes out a ruffian.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris becomes the heir to Carter's fortune but refuses to accept the money; Peter desperately tries to keep the wealth in the family.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie finally says his first word, and it's a swear. When Lois is shunned by the other moms, she tries to figure out where Stewie could have possibly learned it.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and her mom, Babs, have a relaxing spa day, leaving Peter to entertain Lois’ father, Carter. The boys fall for an email scam, and have to go to Africa to get their money back. Meanwhile, Brian pursues Meg’s friend, Patty.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter gets a job as the new night watchman, he and the guys turn Pawtucket Brewery into an after-hours bar. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian's plan to collect "lost dog" return money backfires when Brian is used for a dangerous TikTok stunt.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Brian sustains an injury, Lois gets hooked on his painkillers. Meanwhile, Cleveland refurbishes Peter's ping pong table, which becomes the hot spot in the neighborhood.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Chris go to fat camp where they cross paths with a serial killer who targets overweight kids; an album cover frightens Stewie.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter attends a business conference in Atlantic City with Preston. Meanwhile, Stewie helps Chris prepare for his role in Adam West High's rendition of "Romeo and Juliet".

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian bonds with his girlfriend's son, both Brian and Kyle become fast friends after he defends the young boy from bullies. Peter, Quagmire, and Cleveland become intrigued with Joe's new classic corvette, they take a joy ride in the car without Joe's consent.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie's future life could not be going any better, living as a wealthy, old Mr. Burns-type man, until his retirement is interrupted by Brian, who brings news that Peter is dying.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Principal Shepherd moves in with the Griffins after he is fired for fat-shaming Chris at school. Meanwhile, Brian sues Stewie for writing a series of children's books which portray him as an idiot.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter and Quagmire accidentally switch underwear, they begin to mimic the other's behavior. Stewie and Doug agree to commit each other's murders.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and her father attend the funeral of Lois' childhood nanny. Peter and the rest of the family turn Pewterschmidt mansion into a hotel.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter makes a hologram of himself in order to get out of menial household duties.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After a Christmas tree lightning ceremony Brian crashes into Mayor West's nativity scene causing outrage.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie tries to civilize his half-brother, Boston Stewie, so that he may be adopted. Meanwhile, Meg and Chris start a venture in which people online pay Chris to sit on and smash cupcakes.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian and Peter are required to attend a 12-step program to deal with their drinking problems; Death gives Peter a glimpse of what life would be like without alcohol.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is mistakenly identified as transgender, which leads to an unexpected event and an important learning lesson.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris runs away to South America after a school hazing during his first day of being a freshman. Meanwhile, Peter gets a job at the brewery, where he is allowed to drink on the job, just as long he controls himself. However, Peter's drinking habit gets out of hand, Angela demotes Peter to work at shipping.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffin family decides to start their own restaurant, though have a hard time attracting customers. After Joe promises to help, Peter is excited at the fact of it becoming a local hangout for the police. However, things don't really turn out as hoped, and instead it begins to be popular with the handicapped. When Peter attempts to change the place, a sudden twist of irony quickly changes his mind.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Pawtucket Brewery hires new bosses, who decide to change the company's mascot. In an attempt to be crowned the new face of Pawtucket Ale, Peter and Brian go head-to-head.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Brian accidentally becomes a local hero, an old street pal, and now a successful millionaire dog, reaches out and tries to convince him to get neutered. Meanwhile, Lois gets a pixie cut against Peter's wishes, so Peter retaliates by trying to out-embarrass her.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg starts dating a chicken she meets on a reality dating show.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Ryan Reynolds pays a visit to Quahog and becomes obsessed with Peter Griffin. Meanwhile, Stewie tries to be a big boy and steals the family car for a joy ride but lands in a bit of trouble.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After successfully landing a plane, Brian and Quagmire go on a press tour. Meanwhile Chris and Stewie open a boutique pizza place.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter must gain his family's respect back after he makes a humiliating throw. Brian and Stewie become chiropractors.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Mort bowls a perfect game, and Quagmire gets a key to the city by performing CPR, Peter notices he has nothing to be proud of. He then attempts to achieve fame by setting the world's record for most nickels swallowed. However, a slight miscalculation leaves him blind with nickel poisoning.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: At odds with Peter, Lois takes Quagmire to her high school reunion; the rest of the family must cover up the death of a pizza delivery man.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Joe accidentally sees Lois naked, leading to a reinvigoration of his sex life with Bonnie. Meanwhile, Stewie is gifted an electric SUV and quickly embodies the "Range Rover" lifestyle.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: With the help of an out-of-this-world remote control, Stewie and Brian travel through alternate universes, including a post-apocalyptic world and a parallel world run by dogs where humans are pets.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and the guys become shipwrecked at Fire Island; Chris and Brian perform a lobotomy on a social-media-crazed Stewie.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter hires the town's new lawyer, Brick Baker, to represent him in litigation against his new neighbor -- who turns out to be Brick; Brian and Stewie become lobstermen and save Rupert from the perils of the deep.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: On the night before Thanksgiving, Brian and Peter drunkenly devour the holiday bird, and then must go out to find a replacement. In Peter absence, Chris attempts to step up and become the man of the house.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Brian’s friendship becomes strained after Brian begins dating one of Meg’s high school teachers. Peter finds a replacement friend in James Woods, who is visiting Quahog because of a suggestion to rename James Woods High after Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Meg is erroneously reported to have died in a car crash, she goes along with the story to start a new life elsewhere. Meanwhile, the Griffins experience a life without Meg Griffin.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois books a fun couple’s vacation in the Bahamas. But, much to Peter’s surprise, it turns out to be a marriage counseling program instead. Meanwhile, Carter watches the kids at home and becomes frustrated that they’re too into their electronics to play games the old-fashioned way. So he tries to fix it himself.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In this Griffin-family twist on the popular HBO series, Peter suspects Lois of betraying his trust with Quagmire at a swanky vacation, while Stewie worries that his wealthy new friends plan to sacrifice him in a volcano.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter spends more time with Meg, Lois and the Guys instead of Stewie, Brian and Chris during an apocalypse which was a later found out it was prank. Meg and Lois try to help Peter get the Chris, Stewie and Brian back after he hurt them by calling them boring.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter fills in for Santa at the mall, but lets the power go to his head when he realises just what he can get away with. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian become holiday office-party crashers.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A new boss quells Peter’s favorite part about work: office birthdays. Stewie is put in detention and comes out a ruffian.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter encourages Meg to attend a high school party, where she takes up drinking, which both concerns and delights her father. Meanwhile, Brian begins spying on his neighbors and suspects Principal Shepherd of a crime.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In noir-style, Peter/Mac investigates the disappearance of Meg/Sister Megan.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When the brewery announces it will be firing one employee, Peter is nervous he will be the one let go; the family departs on a Yacht Rock cruise that doesn't go as expected.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter’s couch is stolen from his front lawn, the guys form a neighborhood watch group. While on duty, Peter sees someone trying to climb into Cleveland’s house and he shoots him. Peter is then arrested and charged with a hate crime. Meanwhile, Cleveland is touched when Peter finally admits to the accident, so he takes the fall for the crime and Peter is exonerated.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris is forbidden to see Jerome's daughter Pam, so when the kids run away together, Jerome and Peter must work together to find them.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Cleveland returns to town, but is instantly banned from spending time with Peter when Lois and Donna get into a big fight over parenting.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg deals with a college admissions scandal, while Brian embarks on a fitness journey.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is given the position of the next White House Press Secretary and the Griffin family gets ready to make a move to Washington, D.C.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins tell their own versions of three HBO shows: "Game of Thrones," "Succession" and "Big Little Lies."

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois embarks on a journey of self-discovery.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Meg takes Stewie to the mall to meet Santa, she has a surprising response. Meanwhile, Stewie is entirely scarred from the same experience and Brian tries to help him process it.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To everyone’s surprise, Chris wins homecoming king. However, Meg finds out that the cool kids plan to prank him during the homecoming ceremony, so they go to the school to investigate. Meanwhile, Cleveland starts hanging out with Jerome, the bartender. So Peter and the guys try to replace him.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter makes it through a bad flu season without getting sick, the hospital asks him to volunteer as a nurse, but he finds himself at a crossroads when his mortal enemy, The Giant Chicken, is admitted to the ER. Meanwhile, Brian receives his genetic test results and learns he is part cat, causing him to have an identity crisis.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Impelled by nostalgia, Peter recreates movie scenes from his favorite decade; Stewie and Doug compete to retrieve a Frisbee stuck at the top of the jungle gym.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian become overwhelmed after winning a coffee shop. Peter and his friends annoy Meg at her new lifeguard job when they don't follow the pool rules.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To celebrate Peter and Lois' wedding anniversary, the Griffins present their own versions of three well-known love stories.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and his friends are at the record store and they tell the stories of three rock 'n' roll singers: Jim Morrison, who was the vocalist for The Doors, an American rock band and Jim's sudden downfall. Muddy Waters, an American blues singer, but his credit for his music doesn't get noticed. Elton John, a British singer and pianist, who has a hard time finding acceptance and he learns that seeking his parent's love is meaningless.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Death grants Peter's wish to go back in time to 1984 when he was 18, but only for one night. He ends up bailing on a date with Lois to hang out with a movie actress. When he returns to the present, he soon realizes that everything is anything but normal, as he is now married to the former actress, and Lois is with Quagmire. Meanwhile, Stewie, Chris and Meg have also taken on Quagmire's charming characteristics.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After discovering his checks are actually a large size post-it pad, Peter demands he have access to the checking account. While at the bank, Peter & Lois discover that his first name is actually Justin, so Peter embraces his new partying “bro” lifestyle. Meanwhile, Joe arrests Glenn after he sleeps with an underage girl, which leads to an unexpected reunion.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To take advantage of a work policy granting leave in the event of a pet's death, Peter adopts the oldest bird he can find... and realizes it used to belong to Hitler. Meanwhile, after giving Stewie a signed copy of his book, Brian finds it in a free library, causing Brian to give up writing.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: An accident leaves Peter with a high-pitched voice, so Jesus coaches him into fending off the choir boys; Stewie is attracted to Lois' new masculine physique.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In search of Stewie's cherished big wheel tricycle, that Peter threw out, Stewie and Brian embark on an adventure to "Garbage Island," a giant floating mass of trash out in the sea. Meanwhile, Lois tries to help Quagmire elevate his dating game, as he courts a middle-aged woman.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins attempt to conquer Mount Everest after competitive friends mention they plan to scale the legendary peak. Things, however, are not looking up for the Griffins, as a wicked storm leaves them pinned down on the side of the mountain.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg has fun partying with Peter when he takes her to a college for an interview. Brian must learn how to live in the wild when he gets sprayed by a skunk and is prohibited from entering the house.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian's cosmetic surgery leads him down a new career path as a real estate agent, which gets him into trouble with Quagmire.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Stewie receives a participation medal in his school's toddler games, he begins to question everything he has ever won. Meanwhile, Peter causes a fight between Lois' parents and must repair the damage he caused.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter hears Loretta scream, he tries to help out, but discovers Loretta is cheating on Cleveland. Peter figures out that it was Quagmire and decides to tell Cleveland about it. But when Loretta walks out on Cleveland he doesn't take it lightly.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter secretly gets a credit card and overspends the family money, so Lois grounds him. To plan the perfect escape, he builds a tunnel leading directly to The Drunken Clam.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg is thrilled to be recruited as a model, but it turns out to be for a very specific type of modeling: the foot-fetish industry. Meanwhile, Brian accompanies Stewie on an open-road adventure when Stewie decides he wants to experience life outside of preschool.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter gives Lois’ dad the bachelor party he never had, Mr. Pewterschmidt goes into a coma. Even Dr. House can't help. Lois gains control of Pewterschmidt Industries which she passes to Peter. He gets power-hungry and fires the board. Mr. Pewterschmidt bounces back and tries to regain control of what he’s built.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After a vestigial twin grows out of Peter's neck, which he names Chip, he eventually starts realizing that people like Chip more than they like him, so Peter has him amputated. However, after Peter gets into trouble, he realizes he needs Chip more than ever.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Peter and the guys get caught pretending to be military veterans and are found guilty of the Stolen Valor Act, a judge sentences them to join the U.S. Coast Guard.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Hard Times at Adam West High
programme.desc: Chris uses Stewie's time machine to stop himself from having an erection during a book report. Peter becomes jealous when he sits out of a lottery pool and the guys win.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris leaves home to move in with Herbert while Stewie and Brian become obsessed with singer Anne Murray.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois hypnotizes Peter into satisfying her sexual proclivities. Meanwhile, Stewie renovates his treehouse in an attempt to win over Brian and Chris.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In this follow up to the "Viewer Mail" episodes, the Griffin family retells The Lord of the Rings, explains how the Griffins' ancestors arrived in Quahog, and looks back at Quagmire's 1960s variety show.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: "Family Guy" retells three Oscar-winning stories: "Silence of the Lambs," "American Beauty" and "Forrest Gump".

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg pursues an unlikely friendship with an NFL great; Stewie and Brian host a dinner party at a home furnishing store.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois is blocked from a vacation rental app and accused of stealing after the family's trip to Nantucket. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian buy saltwater taffy in bulk, and try their hands at door-to-door sales in the community.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Wi-Fi goes out in the Griffin house, leaving the family with nothing to do for the evening. In order to keep the family entertained, Peter and Lois decide to tell the kids the story of how they met and fell in love in the '90s.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Carter Pewterschmidt cancels the annual Quahog Christmas carnival, because he hates the holiday, Peter must transform his father-in-law from a Scrooge into a hometown hero in order to salvage the event. Meanwhile, Stewie devises a master plan to get the one and only thing he wants for Christmas.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie becomes obsessed with Lois, after she saves Rupert. Though Lois doesn't take to the new Stewie all too well. Meanwhile, Peter thinks his doctor is molesting him, when he undergoes a prostate exam.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To pay for Stewie’s private school, Lois becomes a postal worker. She finds a letter from Peter that was never sent to his old fling, Gretchen, dated a week before their marriage. When Gretchen tries to come between Lois and Peter, Lois has a plan of her own.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Fast Times at Buddy Cianci Jr. High
programme.desc: Brian becomes a substitute teacher at Chris' school after his teacher wins the lottery. When Brian is moved to teach a class of troubled kids, Chris falls in love with his new teacher, Mrs. Lockhart, who promises to love Chris only if he kills her husband. When Lois finds out, she and Stewie decide to teach Chris a lesson.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: A Few More Ways to Die in the West
programme.desc: In this Griffin family spoof of the Old West, Peter, known as Quiet Burp, and his gang of outlaws agrees to escort a wealthy businessman on a train journey.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: It’s winter in Quahog, and Lois’ parents take Stewie away on a trip. But when Carter falls asleep at the wheel, the family decides it might be time for him to retire. Peter and Lois take the clan to Florida to check out some fine retirement communities, but Carter refuses to go down without a fight.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When the owner of The Drunken Clam is killed, Peter and the guys must find a way to save the bar, after the bank threatens to close it. Meanwhile, Meg gets a part-time job at a funeral home.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After feeling unappreciated by her family, Lois taps into her villainous side and attempts to win "Best Customer" at her favorite coffee shop.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To help Chris ace his ninth grade history class, Stewie and Brian employ the time travel machine for educational purposes. Not surprisingly, all three get stuck in the past.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois decide to go on a second honeymoon. But when Peter crashes the car, he decides to pose as Mel Gibson to stay in his fancy hotel suite, where they find a copy of a "The Passion of the Christ" sequel. They attempt to destroy the film and end up being chased by two priests who went to retrieve the film.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter, Quagmire and Joe visit the Harvard University campus, Quagmire accidentally marries a prostitute. In order to get out of his marriage, Quagmire pretends to be lovers with Peter.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Mayor West raises the drinking age to 50. Because of dog years, Brian is the only one old enough to buy alcohol, so Peter makes him buy all of his booze.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Chris take a road trip to Canada to prove the existence of Chris' girlfriend. Meanwhile, Lois and Meg compete for the attention of a handsome handyman.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian becomes romantically interested in someone and it inspires him to use Stewie's time machine. Strange consequences result from their journey.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris receives a special one-dollar bill as a birthday present, but he quickly loses it and the dollar passes into the possession of everybody in Quahog.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois accidentally set up a cutaway gag together, which somehow transports them into the cutaway itself.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After attending a baseball game, Peter and Chris are inspired to start a memorabilia business. Meanwhile, Stewie tries to combat aging by getting plastic surgery, but ends up taking it too far.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Lois' piano student, Alana, comes to work for the Griffins, Lois is initially glad for the help, until she realizes Alana's intentions are more sinister than she thought.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: The Return of the King (of Queens)
programme.desc: Lois decides to sell the family's television to help pay for Christmas presents, but when Kevin James gets upset over losing Peter as a viewer, he apparates into the Griffin house.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois vacation in Florida, only to discover they've rented a condo in a retirement community. Back at home, Brian helps Stewie take his first shower.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Peter's hair turns white from a ghost-hunting adventure in his house with the guys, his new look grabs the attention of local news anchor Tom Tucker. Under Tucker's guidance, he learns the ways of reporting fake news, and soon becomes a senior crime analyst on Quahog's Channel 5 News. Meanwhile, Brian and Stewie attempt to help Chris improve his dating game.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When the family fails to help Lois with the Christmas shopping, she walks out on the family and the Griffins must try to save Christmas on their own.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg discovers the seasonal delight that is the pumpkin spice latte, but when the limited time offering lapses, she and Lois concoct an elaborate heist to steal the formula.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter gets carried away when the Nielsen people select the Griffins to have their television-viewing habits monitored and he steals a number of the Nielsen boxes in hopes of controlling the airwaves.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Vladimir Putin comes to Quahog after Peter writes to him about an alternate ending to the Russian version of Rocky IV (1985).

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter starts coaching for Quahog's minor league baseball team, he gets constipated during the national anthem and must kneel, unintentionally becoming the face of racial justice.
